title: hippocrepis squamata var. toletana
intro: Hippocrepis squamata var. toletana, also known as the Toletan knapweed or Toletan hairy needle, is a plant species native to South India and Sri Lanka, which is a variant of the Hippocrepis squamata plant. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and treating gastrointestinal problems, due to its rich content of flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids. In Ayurvedic medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction, powder, or infusion to be used in herbal treatments.
